rationale:
Intuition: Multi-zone announce currently loops `announce()` per zone sequentially. Each Sonos receives its TTS WAV at a slightly different moment, so "all five speakers" plays staggered, not synced. Sonos's native answer is `media_player.join`: one zone coordinates, the others mirror playback bit-perfect, then `media_player.unjoin` separates them. HA exposes both services already.

What I'd fix: No MCP tool wraps `media_player.join` / `unjoin`, so the agent has no way to express "play this in lockstep on these speakers." That gap forces the desynced per-zone fan-out.

How: Add `group_sonos(coordinator, members)` and `ungroup_sonos(members)` tools in middleware/agent_tools.py. Extend `announce(zone_entity_id, message)` with an optional `also_play_on: list[str]` — when set, the flow joins members under the coordinator, plays the announcement once on the coordinator, awaits playback, then unjoins. Update the announce tool description so the agent prefers grouped mode for "all rooms / everywhere / whole house" requests.
instructions:
Goal: add Sonos grouping primitives so multi-zone announcements play in sync.

Files to edit: middleware/agent_tools.py only (no HA / kokoro_tts changes).

1. In middleware/agent_tools.py, after the existing `announce` tool registration (around line 424), register two new tools using the same `@_register(...)` pattern already used in this file:

   a. `group_sonos`:
      - description: "Group Sonos speakers so they play in sync. The coordinator is the speaker that drives playback; members mirror it bit-perfect. Use this BEFORE announce/play_music when the user wants 'all speakers / everywhere / the whole house' to play synced. Pair with ungroup_sonos afterward to release the group."
      - input_schema: object with required `coordinator` (string, an HA media_player entity id like "media_player.kitchen") and `members` (array of strings, entity ids to join under the coordinator). No additional properties.
      - impl: `await ha_call_service("media_player", "join", {"entity_id": coordinator, "group_members": members})`; return `{"ok": True, "coordinator": coordinator, "members": members}`.

   b. `ungroup_sonos`:
      - description: "Release Sonos speakers from a temporary group, returning each to independent playback. Call this after a synced announcement or grouped playback finishes."
      - input_schema: object with required `members` (array of strings, entity ids to detach).
      - impl: `await ha_call_service("media_player", "unjoin", {"entity_id": members})`; return `{"ok": True, "members": members}`.

2. Extend the existing `announce(zone_entity_id, message)` tool with an optional parameter `also_play_on: list[str] | None = None`:
   - Update the @_register input_schema to add an optional `also_play_on` array-of-strings property (NOT in `required`).
   - Update the tool description to add a sentence: "To play the same announcement in sync on multiple Sonos zones, pass the additional zone entity ids in `also_play_on` — the function will temporarily group them under `zone_entity_id`, play once, then ungroup. Prefer this over per-zone announce loops for 'all speakers / everywhere / whole house' requests."
   - Implementation: when `also_play_on` is None or empty, behavior is unchanged. When non-empty:
       i.   call `ha_call_service("media_player", "join", {"entity_id": zone_entity_id, "group_members": also_play_on})`.
       ii.  `await asyncio.sleep(0.7)` to let the group settle.
       iii. play the announcement on the coordinator using whatever the existing announce body already does (TTS synthesis + play_media to `zone_entity_id`).
       iv.  after the announcement returns, `await asyncio.sleep(0.3)` then call `ha_call_service("media_player", "unjoin", {"entity_id": [zone_entity_id, *also_play_on]})`.
       v.   wrap steps i–iv in try/finally so the unjoin always runs even on TTS failure.
       vi.  return shape: same as the single-zone case but with an extra `grouped_with: also_play_on` field.

3. Do NOT modify kokoro_tts.py, signal_handler.py, claude_models.py, ha_intents.py, or any HA YAML. Do NOT change the system prompt. Do NOT add tests.

4. Validate with `cd /opt/benson && python -m py_compile middleware/agent_tools.py`.

5. Commit with message: "agent_tools: add group_sonos / ungroup_sonos and announce.also_play_on for in-sync multi-zone playback".

Reference: existing tool pattern is visible in agent_tools.py at the `control_light` (line ~70) and `announce` (line ~424) registrations — match that style exactly (decorator + async impl, return dict with ok=True).
